host_interpretation: resistant and susceptible hosts diverge in defense metabolite routing
mechanism_note: Resistant ZS5 and susceptible Westar show distinct transcriptome-metabolome changes; resistant response includes different IAA and tryptophan-derived defense logic
notes: Strong bacterial comparison because it links metabolic routing directly to resistance contrast.

host_interpretation: resistant state is more coordinated across transcript and metabolite layers
mechanism_note: Resistant soybean line shows stronger PR-gene, JA/ET, MAPK, ROS, and phytoalexin-linked defense program than susceptible line
notes: High-value cross-layer comparator because it connects signaling and metabolite outputs.

host_interpretation: resistance is phased rather than uniform
mechanism_note: Resistant sugar beet shows early containment, mid-stage immune escalation, and late metabolic resilience, while susceptible plants show delayed ROS detoxification and dysregulation
notes: Excellent pattern record because it encodes timing as a resistance feature.

comparison_pattern: massive transcriptomic asymmetry between tolerant and susceptible hosts
host_interpretation: tolerance depends on successful pathway mobilization rather than small expression shifts
mechanism_note: Tolerant rice cultivar triggers thousands of DEGs including PRRs, Ca2+ signaling, MAPK, JA/ET, TF families, while susceptible cultivar shows almost no effective immune induction
notes: Key fungal-context record because the asymmetry is unusually clear.

comparison_pattern: photosynthesis suppression as part of resistant response
host_interpretation: resistance includes costly but coordinated metabolic reallocation
mechanism_note: In tolerant rice, chloroplast and photosynthesis-related genes are downregulated as part of the defense program, unlike the poorly responding susceptible line
notes: Important because it shows resistance is not simply "more growth plus more defense".

host_interpretation: resistance logic extends beyond annual crop systems
mechanism_note: Comparative transcriptome design between susceptible Ponkan Mandarin and resistant Punctate Wampee supports extraction of resistance-associated DEG and pathway differences in a perennial pathosystem
notes: Good broadening record for perennial bacterial disease
column13: though current accessible detail is thinner than other sources.

comparison_pattern: delayed ROS detoxification in susceptible plants
host_interpretation: susceptibility reflects late and poorly coordinated detox/signaling control
mechanism_note: Susceptible sugar beet exhibits delayed ROS detoxification and transcriptional dysregulation rather than phased resistance
notes: Useful negative pattern because vulnerability is not just 'absence of defense' but a recognizable failure mode.
